baseline_driver_top10_msg.title: X-axis (SHAP value)
baseline_driver_top10_msg.description: Represents the impact on the model's predicted value. A value further to the right of 0 indicates a factor that increases the output (in this case, $\Delta$ADAS-Cog).

baseline_driver_top10_msg.title: Color (Feature Value)
baseline_driver_top10_msg.description: Represents the magnitude (size) of the value for that specific variable. High value, Light Blue. Low value.

baseline_driver_top10_msg.title: Dot
baseline_driver_top10_msg.description: Each dot represents one patient. A thicker (denser) vertical accumulation of dots indicates a higher concentration of data points, representing higher frequency and reliability in that specific range.

ADAS Cog 11 BL.title: General Cognitive Assessment Feature
ADAS Cog 11 BL.description: Higher values may indicate greater impairment or increased disease burden, depending on the scoring convention of the specific assessment.

ADAS Cog 11 BL.title: Clinical Relevance
ADAS Cog 11 BL.description: This measure reflects clinically meaningful changes in cognitive or functional performance and may serve as an indicator of disease progression or treatment response.

ADAS Cog 11 BL.title: Assessment Objective
ADAS Cog 11 BL.description: This feature is designed to capture changes in patient status over time by evaluating performance across relevant clinical domains, supporting longitudinal monitoring and subgroup stratification.

ADDRECALL.title: ADAS Word Delay Recall
ADDRECALL.description: Higher scores indicate worse cognitive performance (greater disease severity).

ADDRECALL.title: Early Detection Indicator
ADDRECALL.description: Delayed word recall is used as a highly sensitive indicator for the early detection of the disease, as it is one of the first symptoms to appear in patients with Alzheimer s disease or Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI).

ADDRECALL.title: Core Assessment Goal
ADDRECALL.description: It is a key assessment item that captures memory decline, particularly in the early stages of dementia, by measuring how well one can remember 10 recently learned words after a short period of time.
